title: ResolveTrace standard API responses
description: Shared error and rate-limit response envelopes returned across the ingest surface, including the 429 Too Many Requests body shape.

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse
ErrorResponse.description: Canonical error envelope returned by every ingest endpoint on non-2xx responses, unless a more specific schema is documented (e.g. 429).

message.description: Human-readable message. Never contains tenant-identifying data.

requestId.description: Per-request correlation identifier, echoed from the `X-Request-ID` response header. Include this when contacting support.

details.description: Optional structured details. Shape depends on `error`; consult endpoint docs.

RateLimitClass.description: Which ingest request class tripped the limit.

RateLimitDimension.description: Which dimension of the class was exceeded — request count, event count, or payload bytes.

RateLimitErrorResponse.title: RateLimitErrorResponse
RateLimitErrorResponse.description: Response body returned on HTTP 429 (per-tenant throttle) and HTTP 503 (global shed). Clients should honour the `Retry-After` header and back off with jitter.

retryAfterSeconds.description: Integer seconds until the next request is expected to succeed. Mirrors the `Retry-After` header.

class.description: Which ingest request class tripped the limit.

dimension.description: Which dimension of the class was exceeded — request count, event count, or payload bytes.

RateLimitHeaders.title: RateLimitHeaders
RateLimitHeaders.description: Standard rate-limit response headers emitted on every 429 response (and alongside 200 responses when a tenant is approaching its limit).

Retry-After.description: Integer seconds until the next request is expected to succeed.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: Soft limit for the class/dimension that tripped.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Remaining tokens in the current window (0 on 429).

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix epoch seconds at which the next token is guaranteed available.
